title: A Day Out With Marek
description: Tram to the restaurant, dinner with Marek, and a taxi home.
context: Marek invites you out for dinner. You take the tram there, eat together, and take a taxi home afterward.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Ahoj! Poznám dobrú reštauráciu. Ako sa tam dostaneme?
exchanges.translation: Hi! I know a good restaurant. How should we get there?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks how you should get there. Pick whichever mode of transport you'd choose.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Poďme + mode of transport — correct pattern. Any of these works to suggest how you'll travel together.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Poďme (Let's go) + mode of transport is the pattern. Any of the four options works grammatically.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.translation: Good, let's go by tram. Do you have a ticket?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks if you have a ticket. You don't yet, you need to buy one. What do you say?

choices.translation: Yes, I have a travel pass.
No, I need to buy a single ticket.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Nie, potrebujem kúpiť jednorazový lístok. — correct. Jednorazový lístok = single ticket, from the Tram/Bus lesson.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Nie, potrebujem kúpiť jednorazový lístok is correct since you don't have a ticket yet.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.prompt: Marek reminds you to validate your ticket. What do you say after doing it?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Validoval som ho. — correct. You confirmed you did it, exactly as instructed.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Validoval som ho (I validated it) confirms you've done what Marek reminded you to do.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.prompt: Marek tells you your stop is next. The tram arrives. What do you say as you get off?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Tu vystupujem. — correct. You're confirming this is your stop as you step off.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Tu vystupujem (I'm getting off here) is the right phrase when stepping off at your stop.

exchanges.speaker: waitress
exchanges.translation: Good day! A table for how many?
exchanges.prompt: You arrive at the restaurant with Marek. The waitress asks how many people. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Stôl pre dvoch. — correct. You and Marek together, from the Ordering Food lesson.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Stôl pre dvoch (a table for two) is correct since you're dining with Marek.

exchanges.speaker: waitress
exchanges.prompt: You don't eat meat. What do you tell the waitress before ordering?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Nejem mäso. — correct. Important to mention dietary preferences before ordering, from the Food lesson.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Nejem mäso (I don't eat meat) is the right way to let the waitress know your dietary preference.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks what you'll order. You want the soup. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Dám si polievku. — correct. Dám si is the ordering pattern from the Verbs lesson.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Dám si polievku (I'll have the soup) is the right ordering phrase.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks if the meal was good. It was excellent. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Áno, veľmi chutné. — correct. A genuine compliment for a good meal.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Áno, veľmi chutné (Yes, very tasty) is the right response when you enjoyed the meal.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Je už neskoro. Mali by sme zavolať taxi.
exchanges.translation: It's late already. We should call a taxi.
exchanges.prompt: It's late, Marek suggests a taxi. You agree and need to ask the driver for the address. What do you say once inside?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Na túto adresu, prosím. — correct. Giving the address from the Taxi lesson.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Na túto adresu, prosím (To this address, please) is correct when giving the driver your destination.

exchanges.speaker: driver
exchanges.text: Tu je to. Sedem eur päťdesiat.
exchanges.translation: Here we are. Seven euros fifty.
exchanges.prompt: You arrive home. You give 8 euros and don't need the change. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Drobné si nechajte. Ďakujem! — perfect closing to the night. Tipping politely, from the Taxi lesson.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Drobné si nechajte. Ďakujem! (Keep the change. Thank you!) is the right way to tip and thank the driver.

completionMessage: 🎉 What a day! You chose how to travel, rode the tram, ordered dinner with Marek, and took a taxi home — using everything you've learned about food and getting around, all in Slovak.
